subject: Use lowercase in common.fullname during linting in helm3
commit_message:
Use lowercase in common.fullname during linting in helm3

Helm3 linting check if object name conform Kubernetes naming requirements

Change-Id: I03ac6f4c33048b59d495390a38f4adf6d3afa489
Signed-off-by: Jakub Latusek <j.latusek@samsung.com>
Issue-ID: OOM-2562

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 10 on the queue.

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/5735/

Assignee added: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>

Patch Set 1:

hello,
on this one and https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/112709, I'm not sure it's the right way to just remove issue when linting is in action.

We could maybe try to not have the issue no?

Patch Set 1:

if you look at here: https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//112410-2/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/onap-helm/onap-certInitializer.log
we see that this linting is done on purpose ^_^
Patch Set 2: Published edit on patch set 1.

Patch Set 2:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 6 on the queue.

Patch Set 2:

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/5748/

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> if you look at here: https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//112410-2/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/onap-helm/onap-certInitializer.log
> we see that this linting is done on purpose ^_^

Nope. This chart is not intended to be deployed as is. Only as a requirement from some other component and I expect that this apply to all our charts that we created to wrap global values

Patch Set 2: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/5748/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/oom-master-verify-helm/5748

Patch Set 2:

Gitlab Azure 2: Chained-CI build started, max time: 14400 seconds

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > if you look at here: https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//112410-2/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/onap-helm/onap-certInitializer.log
> > we see that this linting is done on purpose ^_^
> 
> Nope. This chart is not intended to be deployed as is. Only as a requirement from some other component and I expect that this apply to all our charts that we created to wrap global values

you're not supposed to take care of your children :P?

in https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/112410, as it's a release name, the name override is not taken into account and so this doesn't apply yes.
But it breaks helm chart deployment (as we see in the logs).

So I prefer to handle the case by lowering everywhere as it'll force our names to always be DNS compatible.

Another solution would be to replace any capital letter in a word by '-lowerOfTheCapitalLetter'

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 2:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 1:
> > > 
> > > if you look at here: https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//112410-2/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/onap-helm/onap-certInitializer.log
> > > we see that this linting is done on purpose ^_^
> > 
> > Nope. This chart is not intended to be deployed as is. Only as a requirement from some other component and I expect that this apply to all our charts that we created to wrap global values
> 
> you're not supposed to take care of your children :P?

Fortunately they discovered such thing as kindergartens thus as long as my wife is in hospital (visits are forbidden) and my daughter in kindergarten I've a short time window for some own stuff xD

> 
> in https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/112410, as it's a release name, the name override is not taken into account and so this doesn't apply yes.
> But it breaks helm chart deployment (as we see in the logs).
> 
> So I prefer to handle the case by lowering everywhere as it'll force our names to always be DNS compatible.

Well I think that having our common charts with upper name is a good safety measure that prevents people from shooting in their own foot and using them without name override (which would sooner or later fail as we would have a name conflict)

> 
> Another solution would be to replace any capital letter in a word by '-lowerOfTheCapitalLetter'

How about doing this other way.

there is a built-in object .Template.basePath which would be set to common/certInitializer/template while processing certInitializer itself and to "<component>/charts/certInitializer/templates"

how about adding an if and executing this lower only:

{{ if eq (printf "common/%s/templates" $name) .Template.BasePath }}

?

BTW
We would also need then some other measurement that would prevent those charts from being deployed as a part of common but I'd leave that to you how to do this;)
